title: Patch 1.22.1.5 Long-Range Tactics
patchNoteDetails:
## Bugfixes
Fixed a bug where the icon frames for Ranked Match Series 33 rewards and Ranked Match Series 34 rewards had the same design.
Fixed a bug in Solo Mode's Boss Rush stage where the boss Pokémon would grow stronger every time the same stage was cleared.
Fixed a bug in some Quick Battles where the "START" button could not be pressed, preventing battles from taking place.
Fixed a bug in Tournament Mode where the move sets of Pokémon not owned could not be configured, even when the custom rule "Unlock All Unite Licenses" was set.
(Fixed Feb 28) Fixed a bug on the Nintendo Switch version's Pokémon roster screen where changing Urshifu's move set was not immediately reflected.
(Fixed Feb 28) Fixed a bug where the game would lag when the cream from Alcremie's Recover disappeared.
(Fixed Feb 28) Fixed a bug where players would become unable to learn new moves during battle under certain conditions.

title: Patch 1.22.1.2 Bugfix Day
patchNoteDetails:
## Bugfixes
Fixed a bug where the effect of Muscle Band would only trigger once when certain Pokémon attacked twice with a boosted attack.
 
Fixed a bug where Umbreon’s Unite Move absorbed more shields than intended.
 
Fixed a bug where the vortex would appear at Empoleon’s previous location if its Unite Move was used immediately after an empowered Hydro Cannon.
 
Fixed a bug where an unintended delay occurred after Lucario or Mega Lucario performed a boosted attack.
 
Fixed a bug where Mega Charizard Y’s hindrance resistance during Mega Evolution would vanish when hit by specific hindrance effects.
 
Fixed a bug where Dragapult’s Unite Move failed to deal damage to Regieleki under certain conditions.
 
Fixed a bug where Dhelmise’s additional damage of basic attack caused unintended damage to Falinks’ troopers.
 
Fixed a bug where Darkrai’s attacks would fail to hit opposing Pokémon under certain conditions.
 
Fixed a bug where Darkrai could not be hit by attacks under certain conditions.
 
Fixed a bug where Darkrai’s Hypnosis would trigger in the direction of a basic attack under certain conditions.
 
Fixed a bug where Darkrai would deal damage to opposing Pokémon inside the shadow without shadow claws appearing if Shadow Claw was used during Dark Pulse under certain conditions.
 
Fixed a bug where Darkrai’s Dark Pulse would immediately go on cooldown, preventing the second attack from being used right away under certain conditions.

title: Patch 1.21.1.6
patchNoteDetails:
## General Changes
#### Experience System Adjustments
The rules for the amount of experience points you can earn when KOing a Pokémon have been changed.
 

#### If KO'd by one Pokémon
The Pokémon will gain 100% of the experience points provided by that Pokémon
 

#### If KO'd by two Pokémon
Each Pokémon present will gain 65% of the base experience points (for a total of 130%, increasing the total amount gained as a team).
 

#### If KO'd by three or more Pokémon
The total amount of experience points (130%) that would be gained by KOing will be distributed equally among all the Pokémon present.
 

## Held Items
#### Exp. Share
###### HP Stat \[BUFFED\]
HP +240 → +360 (Level 30)
 
###### Passive Bonus \[REMOVED\]
"When the holder and at least one other ally Pokémon not holding Exp. Share gain Exp. Points from knocking out an opposing Pokémon; ally Pokémon not holding Exp. Share gain Exp. Points as if the holder didn't participate in the KO, and the holder separately gains 30% of the total Exp. Points instead. Only 1 holder can gain Exp. Points whenever an opposing Pokémon is knocked out." has been removed.
 

## Pokémon
#### Mega Gyarados
###### Mold Breaker \[NERFED\]
**Defense and Special Defense increase**:
50% → 30%

40% → 30%

When Comfey is attached to an ally, it wasn't possible for Comfey to score alongside the ally.
 

## General Bugfixes
#### Kyogre
###### Scoring
If the effect of Kyogre overlaps with a goal that has been broken by the effect of Regieleki, it was possible to instantly score after the effect of Regieleki has ended.
 

###### HP Recovery Results
Fixed a bug where the amount of HP recovered by the effect gained from KO'ing Kyogre is reflected on the results screen of the Pokémon that KOed Kyogre.

title: 1.21.1.2 The Roar of Sea and Sky
patchNoteDetails:
## Ranked Match
#### Ranking System
Ranked matches have been revamped. No longer using a 3 diamond system to rank up and instead using a point gauge. Excelling in multiple aspects of battle amplifies how fast you rank up. Master rank is unchanged, but now goes from 0-999. A new rank Legend Rank has been added beyond Master Rank. Legend Rank players have priority matchmaking against other Legend Rank player. Falling below the minimum threshold in any rank results in being de-ranked.
 

#### Draft
Draft Matches occur when all 10 players are in Master Rank or higher and the minimum number of licenses for draft are acquired by all 10 players.
 

#### Theia Sky Ruins
Kyogre descends upon Theia Sky Ruins! Ranked matches will take place on Theia Sky Ruins (Kyogre).
 

#### Communication
Fixed a bug where quick chat could not be used on ally goal zones to indicate to defend.
 

## Items
#### Held Items

**Cooldown of gaining additional Aeos Energy** reduced: 20s -> 16s

Shield acquisition condition changed to: "When not receiving Sp. Atk damage for 15 seconds." Every 15s the shield refreshes, this timer is reset every time the user takes SpA based damage and the countdown is visible as a buff icon.

title: Patch 1.16.1.4 Goal Hacker
patchNoteDetails:
#### Battle Item

### Goal Hacker \[INTRODUCED\]
Purchaseable in the Aeos Shop for 4000 Aeos Tickets or 400 Aeos Gems.
 
When used, the user tampers with a singular goal zone within range and the item goes on cooldown. If the item is used on an ally goal zone, it will double the shield provided by the goal zone and speed up it's healing rate (0.5s per heal tick), replacing any non-boosted shield with the boosted one and it will inmediately revert to non-boosted shield values once the item duration expires. If the item is used on an enemy goal zone, it will disable it's HP recovery and shield effects for the duration of the item. This item's effects lasts for 4s regardless if it's used on an ally or an enemy goal zone and it can be used to override the effects of an enemy Goal Hacker. Using multiple ally Goal Hackers on a single goal zone will only refresh the duration of the effect. The boost provided to an ally goal zone can be stacked with boosts from other non-Goal Hacker sources.

### Goal-Getter \[BUFFED\]
**Cooldown** reduced: 75s → 60s

### Slow Smoke \[BUFFED\]
**Cooldown** reduced: 40s → 35s

### Fluffy Tail \[BUFFED\]
**Cooldown** reduced: 25s → 22s

title: Patch 1.14.2.10 Scorching Waves
patchNoteDetails:
#### Pokémon

### Blaziken
The value of 'Blaze' recovery was not matching the effort required to proc 'Blaze' so it's recovery was increased. Focus Blast and Blaze Kick cooldowns have been reduced in order to make it easier to connect between style swaps. This realistically will not affect Blaziken due to the style swap cycling being on a total of 10s cooldown

### Duraludon
While win rate was within normal range, Duraludon's usage rate remained low. Duraludon's play style was a bit too monotonous and one dimensional in taking down Rayquaza as it's primary use. In order to change Duraludon's identity to be less monotonous and one dimensional, Duraludon's damage from basic attacks have been reduced, damage by moves increased, survivability increased and takes longer to KO high HP Pokémon like Rayquaza

title: Patch 1.14.1.2
patchNoteDetails:
## Miscellaneous
Shiny Rayquaza will appear on Battle Map: Theia Sky Ruins from 22-02-2024 to 12-03-2024. 
 
Battle Map: Panic Parade will return 27-02-2024
 
Holowear Effect Spray is now available adding sparkling effects to your Pokémon (purely aesthetic)
  
Draft Pick is implemented at Masters rank when all participating players of a match are above 1400 elo.
  
EX Pokémon limitations have been removed from the ranked system.

## Items
### Muscle Band
Internal cooldown for muscle band has been removed. Pokémon who have multiple hits with attacks or boosted attacks have been reclassified into categories that count towards single hit item procs or not. Scyther/Scizor's Technician 2 hits, Buzzwole's Boosted attack, Rapid Strike Style Urshifu's boosted attacks for example all will proc Muscle Band twice. Azumarill's Play Rough will apply 3x. Internal cooldown being removed technically buffs fast attacking Pokémon such as Cinderace, Razor Leaf Decidueye and Dragapult in late game who's attacks were faster than the internal cooldown of Muscle Band. Charizard is collateral damage and is effectively nerfed.  

See the [FAQ Article](/faq/niche-interactions#MultiInstanceDamage) for a more in-depth breakdown.

### Rapid Fire Scarf
Similar to Muscle Band, Pokémon who have multiple hits with attacks or boosted attacks have been reclassified into categories that count towards single hit item procs or not. Hitting Pokémon with the mentioned ways in Muscle Band's update now increment the counter for Rapid Fire Scarf activation for individual hits as well.  

See the [FAQ Article](/faq/niche-interactions#MultiInstanceDamage) for a more in-depth breakdown.

### Charging Charm
Similar to Muscle Band, Pokémon who have multiple hits with attacks or boosted attacks have been reclassified into categories that count towards single hit item procs or not. Hitting Pokémon with the mentioned ways in Muscle Band's update now charge Charging Charm for individual hits as well.  
See the [FAQ Article](/faq/niche-interactions#MultiInstanceDamage) for a more in-depth breakdown.

## Pokémon
Seems like the theme of this patch is mainly bringing Pokémon up through buffs. This patch seems to be reflected off of the blind pick ladder data rather than competitive scene data where Pokémon such as Buzzwole, Leafeon, Umbreon and Clefable are performing much more highly than their ladder results would indicate.

title: Patch 1.13.1.5

### Lapras
Lapras's Ice Beam had been used more for damage than control and with this change emphasizes hitting with the actual beam for more damage. The change to Water Pulse will make it slightly harder to last hit but Lapras still benefits from staying and maintaining Water Pulse stacks. The Lapras Express was used more for damage than for its utility and so it seems like balance has given up and is nerfing it's cooldown which means even less use for utility and more towards damage. It will still offer pressure to the back line but there will be a bit more breathing room.

title: Patch 1.13.1.2
patchNoteDetails:
MERRY BUFFMAS!! Lot of fan favourites getting love this patch and rightfully nerfs to some. 

#### Items
Yay new toys!
### Charging Charm
Energy is charged through movement and basic attacks. Dealing damage to opposing Pokémon while energy is full will deal additional damage (20/30/40 + 50%/60%/70% Attack). Basic attacks add 9 charge.
 
Stats at level 30
**Attack**: 15
**Movement Speed**: 120

### Resonant Guard
Grants a shield scaling off maximum HP that lasts for 3 seconds to the user and nearby ally Pokémon when dealing damage to opposing Pokémon. This effect goes on cooldown for 10s after it’s triggered.

**HP/5s**: 18

Ratio: 76% SpA -> 58% SpA
Slider: 3 -> 3
Base: 130 -> 120
 
**Damage - per Tick (Light Screen Boost)**: 
Ratio: 98.8% SpA -> 75% SpA
Slider: 4 -> 4
Base: 169 -> 156

###### Unite Move: Mystical Mirage \[NERFED\]
**Damage**
Ratio: 180% SpA -> 140% SpA
Slider: 10 -> 10
Base: 470 -> 375

### Ninetales
Getting some love it needs, the moveset buffs certainly help Ninetales but it'll be a relatively hard sell to have a build that flexes between Aurora Veil and Blizzard. Giving access to the ability to flex between move sets will be good in draft when you can make Ninetales the dedicated auto attack selection but in blind pick, you're probably better off selecting one moveset and sticking to it.

Slider: 1 -> 2
Base: 20 -> 40
 
**Damage Reduction** increased: 30% -> 35%

###### Aurora Veil+ \[BUFFED\]
**Damage Reduction** increased: 35% -> 40%

### Hoopa
Hoopa was extremely slippery and the Phantom Force cooldown didn't start until after returning from the portal. This change makes Hoopa's slipperiness less frequent but it does hinder Hoopa's ability to get vision more easily which is a big factor. Hyperspace Hole's change will be of some impact.
###### Phantom Force \[NERFED\]
**Cooldown** increased: 4.5s -> 6.5s
###### Hyperspace Hole \[NERFED\]
**Cooldown** increased: 10s -> 12s
Movement speed reduced from 110% to 70%

### Tsareena
The Queen is back...? With her defense now fully reverted and some of her problem matchups getting nerfed, she may have a spot in the meta again. The Trop Kick CD getting reduced by 1.5s is massive and I expect to see this as her most played move. Because of Queenly Majesty, any cooldown buff for Tsareena is extra impactful.

### Cramorant
Cramorant is receiving the same improvement that A9 received with Blizzard/Avalanche. Surf/Hurricane now have the same cooldown meaning they can be more easily comboed together. This seemed like a subtle change with A9 but we saw how much that actually changed things. Maybe the same will happen to derpy bird. Also with Spell Vamp, you become more bulky during your Unite move which is also when you unlock the Spell Vamp.

title: Patch 1.12.1.6
patchNoteDetails:
This one surprised all of us as far as timing goes. Some pretty significant changes.
#### Pokémon
### Zacian
No one is surprised to see more nerfs for this Pokémon. Its kit is just too overloaded. This change addresses the burst heavy nature of Sacred Sword toning it down a bit and reducing Zacian's incidental bulk when using it. These changes will certainly bring it closer in line to fair and balanced but Zacian's early critical hit rate is still first and foremost unfun. 
###### Sacred Sword \[NERFED\]
**Damage - Circle Draw:**
Ratio: 80% Atk -> 70% Atk
Slider: 6 -> 6
Base: 130 -> 118
 
**Damage - Flurry (x3 Hits):**
Ratio: 53% Atk -> 46% Atk
Slider: 4 -> 4
Base: 87 -> 80
 
**Damage (Boosted) - Circle Draw:**
Ratio: 104% Atk -> 90% Atk
Slider: 8 -> 8
Base: 169 -> 157
 
**Damage - Flurry (x3 Hits):**
Ratio: 69.3% Atk -> 60% Atk
Slider: 5 -> 5
Base: 113 -> 105
 
**Damage Reduction** reduced: 50% -> 30%

### Mewtwo X
Mewtwo X has still been a problem. These changes look to address that, in particular overall damage and staying power. There probably still need some additional changes but these are still welcome nerfs.
###### Natural Stats \[NERFED\]

###### Psystrike \[NERFED\]

Ratio: 162% Atk -> 146% Atk
Slider: 9 -> 7

### Venusaur
Definitely a buff to the Pokémon but not without potential drawbacks. With level 7 being the evolution, it is possible to be locked out of unlocking the second moves but if one does manage to evolve at 7 quickly, a massive stat boost will be had from the evolution itself. A huge spike in stats especially for Special Attack makes Venusaur even greater a threat at the level 7 mark.

It is overall a buff though.

### Chandelure
Extremely underplayed, Flamethrower is receiving buffs along with Poltergeist in attempts to establish a niche of a zone control and poke type Pokémon.
###### Flamethrower \[BUFFED\]
**Damage:**

Slider: 7 -> 7

**Damage (Explosion):**
Ratio: 132% SpA -> 149% SpA
Slider: 24 -> 24
Base: 630 -> 700

###### Poltergeist \[BUFFED\]
**Cooldown** reduced: 10s -> 7.5s
 
**Slow** from contacting the border or furniture changed from 65% slow for 1s to 65% slow for 1s decaying to a 40% slow over 1 second, then the 40% slow lingers for an additional second.

###### Poltergeist+ \[BUFFED\]
**Slow** from contacting the border or furniture changed from 80% slow for 1s to 80% slow for 1s decaying to a 50% slow over 1 second, then the 50% slow lingers for an additional second.
### Eldegoss
Statistically, Eldegoss has very favourable match ups against most of the roster so it did need nerfs. However, this particular change may not address what the actual issue with Eldegoss could be.

###### Pollen Puff  \[NERFED\]
**Healing**
Ratio: 175.5% SpA -> 150% SpA
Slider: 16 -> 13
Base: 350 -> 300

### Clefable
These changes do nothing to address the fundamental issue with Clefable's Draining Kiss in that you will not heal if you do not deal damage to a target's HP. If Draining Kiss could at least heal for even 50% off of damaging shields, Draining Kiss could be more playable. None the less, this is still a buff.

###### Draining Kiss \[BUFFED\]
**Ratio of HP Restored based on damage dealt** increased: 80% -> 100%
###### Draining Kiss+ \[BUFFED\]
**Ratio of HP Restored based on damage dealt** increased: 100% -> 120%
 
#### Battle Items
 
##### Eject Button \[NERFED\]
Eject Button constantly sees play. It is still ubiquitously one of the best if not the best battle item. This looks to address the overwhelming safety this provided to ranged Pokémon as melee Pokémon were often instead forced to take Full Heal to combat the amount of hindrances within the game.
 
**Cooldown** increased: 70s -> 75s
 
##### Full Heal \[NERFED\]
Full Heal itself was not a problem but more so the speedsters and speedster like Pokémon who have mobility which allow them to go unimpeded with Full Heal's unstoppable and reaching the back line of a team. While hard control is powerful in the game, it could be argued hard control was an answer to how many players ran Full Heal, leading to a scenario of "just have more hindrances so that something will land after the full heal window". A reduction in Full Heal may result in two possible outcomes. A meta where hard control reigns and heavy poke instead is a counter, or a meta where hard control falls off as there are fewer Pokémon who can reach the back line allowing a front line to actually front line without relying on hyper overlap of control.
 
**Cooldown** increased: 50s -> 55s
 
##### Slow Smoke \[BUFFED\]
As if to offer a way counter act divers through soft control, Slow Smoke is being buffed. 
 
**Cooldown** decreased: 45s -> 40s
 
**Attack Speed Slow** increased: 30% -> 50%
 
##### Goal Getter \[BUFFED\]
This item is still pretty weak for competitive play and capitalizes on the chaos of ladder play. However, we may see viable strategies revolving around Goal Getter by experienced and coordinated teams. That being said, these strategies are cautioned against running on ladder as not everyone will be on board and cooperate with this particular style or strategy.
 
**Cooldown** decreased: 90s -> 75s
 
#### Held Items
 
##### Slick Spoon \[NERFED\]
This change will not budge this item's supremacy as the best Special Attack item in the game. This should be on practically all special attackers with few exceptions.
 
**SpDef Penetration** decreased: 10%/15%/20% -> 9%/13%/17%
 
##### Focus Band \[NERFED\]
This change will not budge this item's supremacy as a catch all defensive item but it will emphasize wanting its activation at lower HP thresholds to maximize the missing HP. 
 
**% of missing HP Recovery amount** decreased: 8%/11%/14% -> 6%/9%/12%
 
##### Leftovers \[BUFFED\]
Continue ignoring this item. These changes do not address the fundamental problem of the game punishes you for passivity for activation when you generally would rather be attacking Wild Pokémon for experience.
 
**Out of Combat Recovery rate** increase: 1%/1.5%/2% -> 2%/3%/4%
 
##### Drain Crown \[BUFFED\]
Continue ignoring this item. These changes do not address the fundamental problem of not working on shields and not being enough lifesteal bolstering.
 
**Lifesteal** increase: 6%/8%/10% -> 7%/10%/13%

title: Patch 1.12.1.5

#### Pokémon

### Mewtwo Y
The loss in Sp.Atk and critical strike loss results in a damage loss of 0-3% from levels 1 through 4, a damage loss of 12-13% from levels 5 through 8, and a damage loss of 21%-23% from levels 9 through 15 (these numbers are specifically referencing auto attack damage).

### Blissey
Blissey's Unite Move has always been extremely powerful and it's not surprising it has finally taken a hit. Safeguard and Helping Hand were definitely collateral damage as a part of Mewtwo Y but players were already picking up on the utility of Safeguard against heavy control match ups and for protecting longer ranged allies such as Inteleon preventing interruptions.

Base: 1500 -> 1200

These changes are pretty big reduction in bulk and damage. Bubble Beam's shield should be more appealing now however Lapras's damage and survivability will be hindered greatly across all builds. Lapras should still be a strong pick but much more in line.

### Decidueye
There's a lot of discrepancies for Decidueye but here's what *actually* happened to Decidueye for this patch. Official English notes stated defenses were increased but were not actually changed. Japanese notes indicated a Razor Leaf attack speed change and that has in fact happened.

### Blaziken
I'll take a combo number 5 please. Blaziken suffers from what we have internally coined "Bird syndrome"; that is having a high skill floor. Combined with the presence of an oppressive meta and having a high skill floor, Blaziken was performing poorly in combination with bugs on Fire Punch. Fact of the matter is that Blaziken's skill *floor* is higher than a couple Pokémon's skill *ceilings*. Blaziken did not need these buffs at all (bug fixes we'll accept). Notably, Blaziken's defensive stat buffs only net you a bulk increase of from early game to late game of 2% to 0%. However, what's really going to increase Blaziken's survivability is having a lower cooldown on stance swapping which allows for more Blaze stacks *and* an approximate 20% increase in bulk due to faster stance swap shield values.

Mew's changes here are aimed at lowering the skill floor it seems. The change to Mew's boosted auto attack is a fair one since its damage threshold is reduced but it will do more at base which will also help with Mew's mid game farming. The changes to Solar Beam and more accurately Solar Beam+ punishes players less for missing Solar Beams on multiple targets.

title: Patch 1.12.1.2
patchNoteDetails:
While we all wish for Box Legendaries to be nerfed into the ground and never have to see them again, the general pattern for other changes seem to be going at the balance with a chisel rather than the sledge hammer- which I'm not opposed to. Plus with the introduction of two (really one, just a duplicate for different damage types) held items, there may just be a shake up we need.

#### Hold Items
Cursed Bangle and Cursed Incense has been added to the game.
 
#### Pokémon

### Mewtwo X
More nerfs. Hooray! Future Sight was a bit too much control in duration and frequency of pulling players out of position. This change addresses that. Infinite Psyburn is a little less infinite now. Getting critically hit early and at the fixed attack speed Mewtwo X received while in Mega form thus allowing more rolls for crits was incredibly annoying and unfun. Lowering the crit rate should help with this experience.

###### Future Sight \[NERFED\]
**Cooldown** increased: 7.5 seconds -> 9 seconds
**Stun duration** decreased: 0.75 seconds -> 0.5 seconds

###### Unite Move: Infinite Psyburn \[NERFED\]
**Energy Requirement** increased: 100000 to 120000
**AKA**
**Cooldown** increased: 112 seconds -> 134 seconds
240% Atk + 11 x (Level - 1) + 550 -> 190% Atk + 11 x (Level - 1) + 500

###### Natural Stats  \[NERFED\]
**Critical Chance**:
Level 1: 0 -> 0
Level 2: 0 -> 0
Level 3: 0 -> 0
Level 4: 0 -> 0
Level 5: 10 -> 5
Level 6: 10 -> 5
Level 7: 10 -> 5
Level 8: 10 -> 5
Level 9: 20 -> 10
Level 10: 20 -> 10
Level 11: 20 -> 10
Level 12: 20 -> 10
Level 13: 20 -> 10
Level 14: 20 -> 10
Level 15: 20 -> 10

### Mewtwo Y
Round one! NERF! Future Sight is once again being a problem. Damage is being capped as well as its cooldown being raised. Similarly, Recover's cooldown is increasing which makes controlling the Mega evolve a little bit harder. Finally, Infinite Psyburn is similar to Mewtwo X, not so much infinite now but smaller infinity is still infinity.

###### Future Sight \[NERFED\]
**Cooldown** increased: 8 seconds -> 10 seconds
**Damage cap** decreased: 2000 -> 1500

###### Recover \[NERFED\]
**Cooldown** increased: 10 seconds -> 12 seconds

###### Unite Move: Infinite Psyburn \[NERFED\]
**Energy Requirement** increased: 100000 to 120000
**AKA**
**Cooldown** increased: 112 seconds -> 134 seconds
212% SpA + 14 x (Level - 1) + 680 -> 180% SpA + 12 x (Level - 1) + 580

### Tsareena

The official patch notes indicated 20% shield and 25% healing increases however through rigorous testing and experimentation, UDB has determined the official patch notes are incorrect. What we have noted is that prior patches have always adjusted RSBs percent wise together however for this patch the RSB values have been changed separately with the Base value being decreased. We calculated that if the difference in base values for healing were added to the older values, we *would* see the 25% healing increase that has been written on the official notes.

Still somewhat oppressive, Umbreon's staying power in a fight should be impacted by the introduction of Cursed items however even with those two items, Snarl's uptime to be Hindrance Resistant and the intensity of the stat reduction was still a bit too high. This change should make Umbreon more manageable.

Pikachu's Thunderbolt and Thunder set could be compared to Ninetales's Avalanche Blizzard set with less control and more compressed damage. These changes look to bring Pikachu into contention as a substitute for Ninetales for those who prefer to play the electric mouse. Frankly it's been a while since Pikachu's been taken a look at and this is a good time to nudge him into a decent spot given being a starter and release Pokémon.

title: Patch 1.10.1.3
patchNoteDetails:
There's been a distinct lack of snark in our patch notes as of late, so here I am to be snarky and add some much-needed commentary back. Qril is responsible for the Pokemon commentary so go complain to him if you disagree.

#### Battle Items
Shedinja Doll has been added to the game but is not accessible to be used.

#### Emblems
We didn't report on Ho-oh & Lugia's changes last patch. Seems there wasn't a need to though since Ho-oh and Lugia have been reverted to their former glory. Ho-oh is back to -SpAtk, +Atk and Lugia is back to +SpAtk, -Atk. However both of these emblems have returned with a twist. Ho-oh is now Brown/White and Lugia is now Green/White. Gold Pikachu has also been reverted to -0.6 crit rate rather than -1.2 crit rate. Ho-oh is now Ho-OH and Lugia is now Lug-Yaaas

#### Pokémon

### Zacian

Zacian has been at the top of the metagame for a while. This isn't the nerf to completely kill Zacian, but it'll likely throw a wrench in the winrate. Oh right, snark. Uh... DOGGO BAD.

###### Intrepid Sword \[NERFED\]
**Minimum energy** required: 5 -> 8
 
**Maximum energy** charge: 10 -> 15
 
**Buff duration** reflected of energy changes: 
 
5 energy = 3s -> 8 energy = 3s
 
8 energy = 6s -> 12 energy = 6s
 
10 energy = 10s -> 15 energy = 10s

###### Attack \[NERFED\]
Regular and boosted **Stage Four** consecutive attack leap range reduced. Now both **Stage Four** consecutive attack leap ranges are the same range.

###### Sacred Sword \[NERFED\]
Stun Duration decreased: 0.6s -> 0.2s

### Sableye

I don't really get this change. Sableye wasn't exactly out there tearing up the metagame. Did his bulk really need a 10% nerf? This feels excessive. On the other hand, people *really* hate Sableye, so maybe it's just a Teemo-esque situation.

title: Patch 1.10.1.1
patchNoteDetails:
This patch comes with a lot of bug fixes, an event that we will not be reporting on, and a bunch of neat features. While the official patch notes include bug fixes for more Pokémon, we've only added confirmed bug fixes to our patch notes for the time being. 

## New Features
#### Battle Reports
Battle reports have improved information and now have goal zone destruction information as well.

#### Replays
Replays have been added to the game. You may save up and play up to two replays by default, gaining access to more replays by being a UNITE club member. Great for being able to review your VOD or for content creators who may be playing off-camera but still wanting to show off a game they played after the fact. 

#### Surrender Vote
Automatic decline of surrender vote has been added to the game! Check your settings for further details.

#### Matchmaking
Now when a match is found, a splash screen that shows off your trainer fashion has been implemented.

#### UNITE Club Membership
Added the function to store up to 200 unclaimed gems allowing players to log in once ever 5 days to claim them. Added 2 rank protection cards per month to the membership bundle. When part of the membership, increases the number of replay videos that can be saved to 20

#### Pokémon

### Lapras
###### Whirlpool \[BUG FIXED\]
Fixed an issue where the move continued even after being KO'd

###### Unite Move: Lapras Express \[BUG FIXED\]
Fixed an issue where if a Pokémon was returning to base and was picked up, the movement speed of that Pokémon would keep the movement speed of Lapras during the pick up.

### Cramorant
###### Gulp Missile \[BUG FIXED\]
Fixed a bug where the Arrokuda would sometimes bounce infinitely between two Pokémon, dealing infinite damage or until out of bounce range.

### Glaceon
###### Icicle Spear \[BUG FIXED\]
Fixed a bug where wild Pokémon hit by icicles were taking extra damage due to the icicles dealing their base damage as well as shrapnel damage, effectively doubling or quadrupling at Icicle Spear+ Glaceon's damage to wild Pokémon.

### Clefable
###### Follow Me \[BUG FIXED\]
Fixed a bug where Pokémon affected by Follow Me would have their attacks and moves deal damage to their allies.

### Zacian
###### Intrepid Sword \[BUG FIXED\]
Fixed a bug that sometimes caused consumed Aeos energy to return if Zacian used its Unite Move, Sovereign Sword, immediately after using a boosted move.

### Comfey
###### Triage  \[BUG FIXED\]
Fixed a bug where if Zoroark Illusions into an enemy Pokémon, Comfey would become detached. Zoroark Illusioning into a wild Pokémon will still force Comfey to detatch.

### Chandelure
Fixed a bug where the Burn applied by Moves would proc Shell Bell and Choice Specs. Fixed a bug where the Burn applied by the Boosted Attack would proc Muscle Band's passive effect.

title: Patch 1.9.2.8
patchNoteDetails:
#### Pokémon
Welcome to the patch rundown for debatably the worst patch in Unite history. While there were much needed changes, the quantity (and in a couple cases, quality) of the changes is, in my (LG Lazadin) opinion, nowhere near enough to fix the state of the game as is. With that being said, here's the full formulas below~

### Comfey
Comfey has been an extremely toxic, non interactive mon ever since its hotfix buffs- providing amazing single target healing and some of the most consistent cc in the game while being completely invulnerable. These changes tone down its consistent healing (especially if you aren't picking up flowers!) as well as lower the burst healing of the unite move, and tone down the CC and damage provided by Magical Leaf. Comfey is still gonna be a lot stronger than its release version, but hopefully much more reasonable.
###### Floral Healing \[NERFED\]
**Healing** 
Ratio: 115% SpAtk -> 97% SpAtk
Per Level: 0 -> 0
Base: 350 -> 300

**Overheal Shield**:
Ratio: 57.5% SpAtk -> 48.5% SpAtk
Per Level: 0 -> 0
Base: 175 -> 150

###### Magical Leaf \[NERFED\]
**Stun time** Decreased: 0.75 seconds-> 0.5

**Damage - per Leaf**: 
Ratio: 20% SpAtk -> 16% SpAtk
Per Level: 2 -> 1
Base: 80 -> 60

###### Unite Move: Flowery Fields Forever \[NERFED\]
**Empowered Floral Healing Healing**:
Ratio: 172.5% SpAtk -> 126.1% SpAtk
Per Level: 0 -> 0
Base: 525 -> 390

**Empowered Floral Healing Healing - Additional per Flower**:
Ratio: 22.5% SpAtk -> 19.5% SpAtk
Per Level: 0 -> 0
Base: 68 -> 59

**Empowered Floral Healing Overshield**:
Ratio: 86.25% SpAtk -> 63.05% SpAtk
Per Level: 0 -> 0
Base: 263 -> 195

**Empowered Floral Healing Overshield - Additional per Flower**:
Ratio: 11.25% SpAtk -> 9.75% SpAtk
Per Level: 0 -> 0
Base: 34 -> 30

### Espeon
Espeon is another good change, hitting its psyshock damage by between 16% and 17% at every level regardless of items etc- tuning its damage down to a point where hopefully other mages can compete, however its still by far the single strongest secure in the game at level 4 and once the espeon players have adjusted to the new damage value don't expect lanes to be much easier. 
###### Psyshock \[NERFED\]
**Damage - First Hit**: 
Ratio: 79% SpAtk -> 66% SpAtk
Per Level: 15 -> 13
Base: 410 -> 340

**Damage - Subsequent Hit**: 
Ratio: 23.7% SpAtk -> 19.8% SpAtk
Per Level: 5 -> 4
Base: 132 -> 102

### Lapras
Let's see... Blue... lots of AoE... unique trait on unite move... tanky... self sustain... CC... broken passive... where have I heard this before? Lapras has quickly shown to be one of the strongest releases, and a good part of that is its damage combined with its sustain. Hitting its damage a bit (14% ish) while still allowing it to be a tanky monster is a nice start, but I'd expect to see Lapras dominating all levels of play next patch regardless. 
###### Water Pulse \[NERFED\]
**Damage - First Pulse**: 
Ratio: 117% SpAtk -> 100% SpAtk
Per Level: 15 -> 13
Base: 340 -> 290

**Damage - Subsequent Pulses**: 
Ratio: 58.5% SpAtk -> 50% SpAtk
Per Level: 8 -> 7
Base: 170 -> 145
###### Lapras Express
**Bugfix** fixed a bug where under certain circumstances, Lapras Express would deal its damage twice

### Cinderace
Well, eventually if you slap enough stats onto a self root that nukes it'll be viable. Feint's buff may also allow for some cool plays, nothing really to complain about there. Cinderace's unite move also gets a LONG requested change in the form of unstoppable, giving it another way to outplay CC as well as a much more consistent execute! Combine that with the much, MUCH lower cooldown now (-38s!) and you may see some rabbit hopping up from time to time.
###### Pyroball \[BUFFED\]
Ratio: 273% Atk -> 345% Atk
Per Level: 26 -> 32
Base: 660 -> 820
###### Feint \[BUFFED\]
**Cooldown** reduced: 11s -> 9s

###### Unite Move: Blazing Bicycle Kick \[BUFFED\]
**New** Cinderace is now unstoppable while casting
**Aeos Energy Requirement** decreased: 135000 -> 100000
AKA
**Cooldown** decreased: 150s -> 112s

### Scizor
Bullet Punch's cooldown adjustment gives Scizor much needed sustain, damage, and makes it slightly less vulnerable to CC! The boosted attack change is also nothing to write off, allowing scizor to hit ~60% physical reduction while fully stacked in lategame fights. However, all in all Scizor was by far the lowest winrate pokemon in the entire game last patch and this kind of change will likely not be enough to send it soaring to the top. Jen is calling.
###### Boosted Attack \[BUFFED\]
**Defense bonus** per stack increased: 25% -> 40%

###### Bullet Punch \[BUFFED\]
**Cooldown** reduced: 6s -> 5s

### Goodra
Goodra was already in a pretty fine spot honestly, the changes to Dragon Pulse are nice for playing Goodra in supportive roles or with minimal sustain otherwise- but making it into a fat white Pikachu with the unite move cooldown change might have been just a little bit too far. 
###### Dragon Pulse \[BUFFED\]
**Healing** added flat healing:
+0% MaxHP + 8 x (Level - 1) + 30

**Healing (Gooey)** added flat healing:
+0% MaxHP + 8 x (Level - 1) + 30

###### Unite Move: Right as Rain \[BUFFED\]
**Aeos Energy Requirement** decreased: 100000 -> 80000
AKA
**Cooldown** decreased: 112s -> 89s

### Glaceon
I'm baffled, genuinely. This set already saw competitive play before being disabled due to a bug (that they didn't fix this patch, lol) and was all in all pretty strong, but now they just made it way easier and more consistent to play with a slight damage buff to boot? Why?
###### Icicle Spear \[BUFFED\]
**Cooldown** reduced: 5.5s -> 5s
 
**Damage - per Ice Crystal**:
Ratio: 34% SpAtk -> 37% SpAtk
Per Level: 3 -> 5
Base: 110 -> 100

###### Ice Shard \[BUFFED\]
**Attack Speed Bonus** increased: 25% -> 50%
**Duration** increased: 2s -> 2.5s
 
###### Ice Shard+ \[BUFFED\]
**Attack Speed Bonus** increased: 50% -> 65%
**Duration** increased: 2s -> 2.5s

### Ninetales
Matching Blizzard's cooldown to Avalanche's is a very nice QoL change for MageNine enthusiasts out there, combined with a nice little unite move cooldown boost to allow you to burst people more frequently and consistently. 
###### Blizzard \[BUFFED\]
**Cooldown** reduced: 9s -> 7s
 
Area **Slow** intensity increased: 20% -> 35%
###### Unite Move: Snow globe \[BUFFED\]
**Aeos Energy Requirement** decreased: 120000 -> 100000
AKA
**Cooldown** decreased: 134s -> 112s
### Lucario

Cooldown

### Chandelure

Fixed bug(s) causing extra effects to occur.

status conditions

### Gengar
###### Dream Eater \[BUGFIX\]
Fixed bug(s) causing one or more effects not to trigger.

### Dodrio 
###### Jump Kick \[BUGFIX\]
Fixed bug(s) that affected the following:
Activation Timing

### Zacian
Hello full patch note reader or control + f enjoyer, I regret to inform you that Zacian's only "nerf" this patch was at the top of the notes, in the form of Comfey nerfs. Zacian is still fully capable of one shotting on level tanks with properly played combos, or just one shotting anything by facerolling. Have fun.

title: Patch 1.9.1.5
patchNoteDetails:
#### Pokémon
Remember that some Pokémon end up being more than the sum of their parts, sometimes they're products. So making changes that seem small could have a large impact than you imagine. This patch is full of those types of changes and repurcussions will vary and have to ultimately be seen in action. 

### Sableye
Sableye's control is still a bit too much. These changes should give a window to act (similar to how Slowbro Surf+ formerly gave no time to react but was given moments where you could act)
###### Knock Off \[NERFED\]
First activation **incapacitation duration** decreased: 0.5s -> 0.4s
 
First activation **incapacitation duration** for no Aeos energy decreased: 1s -> 0.6s
 
**After-Hit Reactivation time** reduction: 7.5s/8s -> 6s
 
Second activation **incapacitation duration** decreased: 0.8s -> 0.6s
 
**Incapacitation duration** for no Aeos energy decreased: 1.2s - 1s
 
**Cooldown** increased: 7.5s -> 8.5s

###### Confuse Ray \[NERFED\]
**Duration** decreased: 2.5s -> 2s

### Zacian
It is no secret that Zacian needed changes. These were desperately needed. Will they be enough? Time will tell. However these changes do suggest Zacian will be slower starting which gives players time to fight and contest farm from lanes and actually getting a chance to retreat. Furthermore, without Agility's cleanse, Zacian can be actually chain controlled and KO'd. This will probably force Full Heal to be used rather than having the freedom of mobility from Eject Button.

Now that players have learned to play with increased CD, and with Slick Spoon's introduction, Glaceon's Icy Wind is doing a bit too much, particularly early game for snowballing. title: Patch 1.9.1.2
patchNoteDetails:
# MAIN SITE UPDATES WILL BE DELAYED FOR 24 HOURS DUE TO DEV TOOLS BEING OUT OF COMMISSION. PLEASE HAVE PATIENCE

## It's Pokémon Day!!

#### Boss Rush (Returns!)
Boss rush returns! Compete against other squads to climb the boss rush ladder. New bosses to take down!

#### Emblems
The remainder of Johto has been added as Boost Emblems along with a special addition - Zacian. A brand new colour has been introduced for emblems: Gray. Bonuses for Gray occur at 3, 5 and 7 and offer damage reduction. 

#### Held Items
### Slick Spoon
Another Special Attack item that seems good in practice, however Special Attack Pokémon will have a a difficult time fitting onto due to the utility choices and desires of Special Attackers builds already.
 
Stats at Level 30
 
**HP**: 210
 
**SpAtk**: 30
 
Ignore 20% of SpDef when hitting Pokémon with Special damage

### Rescue Hood
A support oriented item that is extremely niche. It is expected to only have high impact on Comfey.
 
Stats at Level 30
 
**Def**: 30
 
**SpDef**: 30
 
Increases outgoing Heal and Shield values by 10%

### Rusted Sword
Zacian only item. 

#### Pokemon

### Zacian

Added to the game.

### Delphox
Delphox's Mystical Fire is by far the most popular move choice due to damage output being a product of its damage and comparative cooldown to Fire Blast. This patch aims to rebalance such that Mystical Fire & Flame Charge set's overall damage is reduced while propping up Fire Spin & Fire Blast. Furthermore, Delphox's Mystical Fire & Flame Charge combination was a little too safe. This change will punish players for missing their Mystical Fire's since Flame Charge on its own will not afford the same level of security. These changes will affect the damage output of the move combination however will not affect the mobility for players who are hitting Mystical Fire using Flame Charge's freedom to move while casting.

### Gardevoir
Gardevoir saw a rise in popularity with the damage changes it received as part of its battle pass buffs previously. However with a stronger early game, its already strong mid game became more potent. These changes aim to reduce the oppressiveness of Gardevoir being able to constantly throw out Moves giving a bit more breathing room.

### Espeon
Espeon is a huge winner this patch. One of Espeon's weaknesses is its safety in compositions and fights. These changes allow Espeon to be more proactive in fishing for engagements and using the time garnered to follow up with additional damage as well as stepping up positionally which is a huge boon towards the Stored Power & Future Sight set.

Dark Pulse is far and away the strongest move chosen by Tyranitar players due to its capability of moving while performing the Move. Ancient Power is a vastly popular move that offers mobility and shielding. Meanwhile, Stone Edge has always been incredibly weak due to the clumsy animation and requirement of staying still. This patch, changes to Ancient Power and Dark Pulse are aimed at reducing their efficacy while rewarding Stone Edgers for actually hitting Stone Edges.

title: Patch 1.8.1.6
patchNoteDetails:
#### Pokemon

### Sableye
Shadow Sneak provided too much safety especially into compositions that lacked ways of hindering Sableye. This change specifically targets Shadow Sneak's duration to stealth and restealth. 
###### Shadow Sneak \[NERFED & BUFFED?\]
**Duration until stealth when activated** increased: 0.75s -> 1.5s 
Now displays with a count down bar.
 
Site description will be updated to more accurately reflect mechanics of Shadow Sneak

### Urshifu
Urshifu's dominant position in the game is a result of the defensive utility of Single Strike Style's moves as well as Wicked Blow's damage and easy to pilot play style. These changes reduce Urshifu's utility and defensiveness in hopes of providing enemies with a greater opportunity to knock out the Urshifu. Single Strike Urshifu's Unite was also problematic in both flavour and function. Ebon Fist's secondary mode to displace is hardly used due to the fact that a no input Ebon Fist was still area effect damage and the control aspects of the move were not limited to just the main target.
 
On the other hand, Rapid Strike Style has been suffering for many a reason. These changes look to reduce the down time in Rapid Strike Style's gameplay and allow it to play in a manner more befitting of its style name
###### Wicked Blow \[NERFED\]
**Damage Reduction while Charging** decreased: 30% -> 20%
 
###### Wicked Blow+ \[NERFED\]
**Damage Reduction while Charging** decreased: 60% -> 40%
 
###### Unite Move: Ebon Fist \[NERFED\]
**Damage:**
Ratio: 440% Atk -> 330% Atk
Per Level: 17 -> 12
Base: 820 -> 615
 
Non input Ebon Fist only knocks up the main target.
###### Boosted Attack \[BUFFED\]
**Cooldown reduction** on-hit for Surging Strikes increased: 1.5s -> 2s
 
###### Surging Strikes \[BUFFED\]
**Cooldown** decreased: 13s -> 11s
 
###### Unite Move: Flowing Fists \[BUFFED\]
**Number of Attacks to add to the consecutive blow counter** decreased: 3 -> 2

### Scizor / Scyther
Scizor is getting a touch in part due to its pre-evolution Scyther. Scyther on the other hand deserves the changes. This is in large part due to Scizor not having Cooldown Reduction base stats versus Scyther but Scizor was also doing a little too much on its own while being very difficult to take down especially with physical damage. Officially, Scyther's nerfs are a result from it being too mobile.
###### Swords Dance \[NERFED\]
**Cooldown** increased: 6s -> 7s 
 
**Attack Buff** reduced: 15% -> 12%

###### Dual Wingbeat \[NERFED\]
**Cooldown** increased: 5s -> 6s 

###### Unite Move: Green Illusion Dive
**Targeting Range** decreased by 30%
 
**Dash Range** decreased by 20%

### Mr. Mime
Still having some of the best damage and control in the game, Mr. Mime is once again targetted here in its raw damage. Mr. Mime's conditional damage remains the same as an encouragement to push for Barrier & Confusion as a moveset to be taken together. 
###### Confusion \[Nerfed\]
**Damage:**
Ratio: 175% SpAtk -> 148.75% SpAtk
Per Level: 16 -> 14
Base: 400 -> 340
 
***Conditional damage against walls or power swapped targets has no change***

###### Confusion+ \[Nerfed\]
**Damage:**
Ratio: 191% SpAtk -> 163% SpAtk
Per Level: 21 -> 18
Base: 460 -> 390
 
***Conditional damage against walls or power swapped targets has no change***

###### Power Swap \[Nerfed\]
**Healing**
Ratio: 20% SpAtk -> 16% SpAtk
Per Level: 2 -> 2
Base: 15 -> 12
 
**Movement speed** while tethered reduced to: 15% -> 8%

###### Unite Move: Show Time \[Nerfed\]
**Damage (First 3 hits):**
Ratio: 79% SpAtk -> 72% SpAtk
Per Level: 5 -> 4
Base: 220 -> 200
 
**Damage (Final hit):**
Ratio: 158% SpAtk -> 144% SpAtk
Per Level: 10 -> 8
Base: 440 -> 400

### Clefable
Clefable's Moonlight and Gravity impact is difficult to quantify. Moonlight encourages grouping which can be dangerous while Gravity is extremely reactive. These changes will allow Clefable to heal more powerfully so that even with less ticks together, Clefable can still heal enough and give Gravity more impact for using it correctly

###### Moonlight \[BUFFED\] 
**Healing per tick:**
Ratio: 60% SpAtk -> 70% SpAtk
Per Level: 0 -> 0
Base: 40 -> 45

###### Gravity \[BUFFED\] 
**Stun Duration** increased: 0.5s -> 1s

### Hoopa
Ever since Theia Sky Ruins's introduction, Hoopa has been in a weaker spot in part due to the amount of experience that teams can take such that fast rotations through Hyperspace Hole have had less value since you would otherwise like to run through center picking up wild Pokémon on the way. In combination with this is the base healing changes. Hoopa's getting some major changes this patch that is likely to bring it out as one of the best supporters again.

###### Phantom Force \[BUFFED\]
No longer requires enemy or allied player targetting. Instead Hoopa may target the ground as a standard Area of Effect move.

###### Hyperspace Hole \[BUFFED\]
**Cooldown on portal usage activation** decreased: 15s -> 10s
 
**Base Healing activation from using Hyperspace Hole** decreased: 0.5s -> 0s

###### Unite Move: Rings Unbound \[BUFFED\]
**Aeos Energy Requirement** decreased: 120000 -> 100000
AKA
**Cooldown** decreased: 134s -> 112s

### Dragapult
Released in a fairly balanced state, Dragapult saw much more usage of Dragon Breath. This change pushes more power into Shadow Ball. 

###### Shadow Ball \[BUFFED\]
**Damage:**
Ratio: 70% Atk -> 79% Atk
Per Level: 4 -> 5
Base: 190 -> 210
 
**Damage - Mark Proc:**
Ratio: 14% Atk -> 15.8% Atk
Per Level: 1 -> 1
Base: 38 -> 42
 
**Damage - Final Mark proc:**
Ratio: 70% Atk -> 79% Atk
Per Level: 4 -> 5
Base: 190 -> 210
 
###### Shadow Ball+ \[BUFFED\]
**Damage:**
Ratio: 77% Atk -> 86.9% Atk
Per Level: 4 -> 6
Base: 209 -> 231
 
**Damage - Mark Proc:**
Ratio: 15.4% Atk -> 17.38% Atk
Per Level: 1 -> 1
Base: 42 -> 46
 
**Damage - Final Mark proc:**
Ratio: 77% Atk -> 86.9% Atk
Per Level: 4 -> 6
Base: 209 -> 231

title: Patch 1.8.1.2
patchNoteDetails:
Mathgeek has been consumed by the nerd battlepa- I mean Advent of Code so I'm gonna try writing a little bit of witty banter for this one ~Ven

Special thanks as always to ElChicoEevee and the Mathcord Contributors for the swift and (hopefully) accurate patch notes!
 
#### Gamemodes
### Draft Mode \[NEW\]
Excited to see it coming! I know a lot of people are nervous about draft but if/when it comes to ranked it will definitely be a net positive. 

Draft mode has been added as an option for custom and tournament mode lobbies.
 
#### UI Improvements
Added many features to the UI, including new status icons, a new rewards screen, and stacking indicators for Attack Weight/Aeos Cookie/Spatk. Specs! This is by no means an exhaustive list, so have fun with your QOL changes in game!
 
#### Unite Squads
Added daily and weekly squad missions, alongside a new rewards track with new rewards including a silver emblem box!
 
#### Emblems
The Johto region joins unite in the form of 50 new emblems! Please be patient as the new emblems may or may not cause major changes to the old builds.
 
#### Quality of Life
Formerly it would not be possible to recall if an action to select a move was active. This has now been rectified.
 
#### Battle Items
### Full Heal \[NERFED\]
Full Heal covers up the weaknesses of a lot of the high mobility, semi-tanky, high healing characters- though I must admit, I'm a bit confused why we didn't see an X Speed nerf as well.
**Cooldown** increased: 40s -> 50s
 
**Unstoppable duration** decreased: 2s -> 1.5s
 
#### Held Items
### Drain Crown \[NEW\]
A new flavor of defensive item for basic attack based Pokemon- please note that it will only heal off of **physical** basic and boosted attacks, do not build this on Special Attackers.

Stats at 30
**HP**: 120
**Attack**: 18
The user gains 10% Lifesteal

### Rapid-Fire Scarf \[NEW\]
Our second ever Attack Speed item! Works best with characters that have strong, but time limited auto attack buffs such as Alolan Ninetales- though before equipping it, I would highly recommend you ask your local math nerd about how it might (or might not!) help your favorite character. Link to the Mathcord can be found in the "Site Affiliates" section at the bottom of this page. 

Stats at 30
**Attack Speed**: 9%
**Attack**: 12
After attacking 4 times, the user gains 30% attack speed for 5 seconds. This effect has a 10 second cooldown upon activation.

### Experience Share
This item has functioned this way since launch so... it'll be interesting to see how this shakes up the support meta, though I do wonder how its gone this long without being noticed if this was how it was always intended to work..?

**Bug Fix** Fixed a bug where the boosted experience to allies portion of the item wasn't activating unless the holder was the lowest EXP on the team. The passive EXP portion still requires the holder to be the lowest exp.
 
#### Pokemon
### Urshifu \[NEW\]

Do you prefer Reliable Excavation Demolition or Builders League United?

###### Added to the game

### Sableye
Boasting the strongest patch 1 winrate in recorded history and a nearly flawless record in the few tournaments it was allowed in, theres no doubt about it- Sableye is very powerful. Hopefully the changes will hurt the more frustrating aspects of the character- being the extremely high burst for a support and the extremely difficult to counterplay first rotation invades- without changing its more creative uses. Oh, and they buffed the landmines. Have fun picking up points!

###### Prankster \[NERFED\]

**Cooldown** increased: 11s -> 13s

###### Confuse Ray \[NERFED\]
**Cooldown** increased: 7s -> 8s

### Mr. Mime
This change puts mime at about greedent's tier of HP and defenses, though with drastically different survivability tools. Should do a little bit to hurt it but it by no means removes the pick, especially given the ranged nature of its primary build- Confusion/Barrier.

Zoroark saw a small hit after the cooldown nerfs on Night slash last patch, but its reward for pulling off the combo was still extremely high. This change shouldn't do much to hurt its ability to kill squishy characters (sorry red backgrounds) but it does mean that it may struggle to kill tankier characters, and it hurts its ability to secure objectives. The shadow claw nerf also *maybe* makes the feint attack stunlock combo impossible (possibly moving it towards cut), though it would need to be verified.

The stored power buff on the other hand is very interesting, and helps espeon's ability to kite against dive heavy teams.

### Charizard
I guess they wanted Tyranitar to be the only one with on demand true damage? Not sure I understand this one.

title: Patch 1.7.1.11
patchNoteDetails:
We've got a hefty one for you! Big ups to the Mathcord crew who churned all of this out in record time. Most of these changes are basically exactly what the doctor ordered (with one notable exception, right below). This should inspire a little positivity in the growing health of this game.
 
#### Map: Theia Sky Ruins
 
As everybody knows, what Theia Sky Ruins needed was to be more like Remoat Stadium. That was definitely the number one complaint.

**Baltoy**: Two of the center four baltoys have been removed. North and South have been removed.
 
**Rayquaza**:

Clefable was a tad too paper-like. They were a potent threat when unchallenged, but if focused on would melt like snow in Jamaica. A small buff to defenses and an increase to immediate potency means that getting shredded won't be as bad, since you'd have had a bigger impact - and it makes you a bigger threat to take down.

Slowbro's primary role in the game is to take Telekinesis and to stop the opponent from doing things. They are the "Dispel Magic" of Pokemon Unite. "No, you don't get to do your thing today". Unfortunately, once that gimmick is expended, Slowbro lacked much else to actually do. Telekinesis, Unite Move, Telekinesis, hope the fight is over. Unfortunately as well, Slowbro wasn't quite tanky enough to survive the whole barrage in a 3v2 situation. Slowbro is supposed to make a 3v2 into a 2v1, but if they focus on him, it becomes a 3v1 pretty quickly. This should give him better teamfighting options, and allow more control of the battlefield.

title: Patch 1.7.1.9

Sit tight cause this one's a doozey. 

### Zoroark

My wallet cries.

###### Added to the game

### Mr. Mime

Mr. Mime's Confusion-Power Swap combination was incredibly powerful. However, nerfing Confusion alone would make Confusion as a whole weaker. This change lets Confusion keep its power when used with Light Screen and Barrier but reins in the power of Confusion-Power Swap.

### Gengar
Gengar's role as a speedster has always been at odds with other speedsters. Where as other speedsters commit more to being melee, Gengar's Dream Eater Shadow Ball moveset allowed Gengar to play more of a poke centric playstyle. Gengar should still fare fine for damage even with natural stat nerfs but will have a greater risk with reduced ranges.

### Tsareena
Trop Kick according to meta statistics had been frequently picked totalling around 86%. Due to Trop Kick's ability to lower attack, it makes sense for Trop Kick's shield to get nerfed and being packed with utility already - a slight nerf to the wake is entirely reasonable.

### Absol
Absol is an early game dominant powerhouse that frequently gets compounded in power given the Super Luck ability. Normalizing the natural attack stat is a light tap down that accounts for the expected damage value for Absol's critical centric kit. Sucker Punch was a little too good at chasing when the key move to be intended with its use Pursuit is already heavy on mobility. Sucker Punch's reduced range will make it even less appealing to run with Night Slash.

### Aegislash
Aegislash's early game as Honedge is actually very strong, especially in a non contestion as an invader. Shadow Sneak changes should result in a slower time arriving in enemy central areas. Once in the enemy central area, due to Honedge's leveling curve, and the ability to fully negate a move of the opponent made Honedge's invasion significantly safer than other Pokémon. The change to Iron Defense and second move learn level should create more risk for invading Honedges. Aegislash's late game with Sacred Sword proved itself to be extremely potent at taking down even the bulkiest members of the roster. A reduction of Defense piercing will still make Aegislash capable of fighting bulkier Pokémon but will need a longer prolonged engagement.

### Mew
Mew doesn't have any phase in the game that is considered weak. These changes hopefully will provide an avenue to combat Mew making the early game slightly weaker and reducing efficiency in later game with reduced resetting potential. Mew has also been incredibly safe with both Agility and Mystical Mirage at Mew's disposal. With Agility's individual movement speed being reduced, Mew will have fewer windows of escape, with Sychronize being the lion's share of the movement speed now.

You would think Venusaur would do well on Theia Sky Ruins due to the multiple choke points which would allow for a heavy AoE Pokémon to do well in. However, due to Venusaur's typical range, Venusaur also gets caught in these choke points allowing for longer ranged Pokémon to supercede it. Venusaur is receiving some much needed defensive stat increases for both sets and is carving out more of a niche with Sludge Bomb's major slow. Petal Dance is also receiving changes which will provide a higher uptime. Finally, Venusaur's Unite is receiving energy requirement changes which should allow for Venusaur's Unite buffs to shine more frequently as well as providing more power in the kit as a whole.

title: Patch 1.7.1.7
patchNoteDetails:
Welcome to another glorious patch! We've got a bunch of changes to throw at your today, so buckle up!
 

#### Theia Sky Ruins

The map is getting massively nerfed, so hopefully this can slow the roll of snowball carries. Also, Regice works now, which is amazing!

##### Central Area
* Accelgor and Escavalier base EXP decreased from 300 -> 230
Laz note: this matches the old values for red/blue buff on Remoat Stadium
* Xatu EXP decreased from 300 -> 250
Laz note: Xatu EXP is static, unlike most wilds
* Altaria Aeos Energy 5 -> 4 (7 -> 6 in final stretch)
* Baltoy changes are unknown still.

##### Top and Bottom Lane
* Indeedee Aeos Energy 6 -> 5 (8 -> 7 in final stretch)
* Indeedee base EXP decreased from 200 -> 160
* Altaria Aeos Energy 5 -> 4 (7 -> 6 in final stretch) 

##### Objectives
* Regice Buff now heals 5% of your max HP every 3 seconds (Both in and out of combat!)
* Rayquaza base Health decreased from 9200 -> 7720
* Rayquaza Health growth reduced from 1600 every 30s -> 1393 every 30s.
* Regice / Regirock / Registeel /Regieleki EXP growth reduced from 30 every 30s to (20/10/10/10/20/10/10/10/10/20/10/10/10/40/30/40/30/40/20/10)
Abu note: Now Regieleki neutral and soldier give the same Exp.

#### Pokemon

### Absol

Yes, Absol. He who needed a nerf.

###### Pursuit:
Buff duration 5s -> 3s

### Aegislash

Aegislash was a bit too consistent with Shadow Sneak, so a little knock to the knees should give it slightly better counterplay.

###### Shadow Sneak
Damage - 2 Hits:
Ratio: 122% -> 103%
Per Level: 11 -> 9
Base: 200 -> 170

###### Iron Head
Increased the cooldown reduction from blocking damage. 2.2s -> 3s

### Azumarill

Azumarill was pretty garbage in the previous patch. They were too consistent to counter by simply staying with a teammate, and lacked the ability to work strongly in the late game because of it. Lowering levels of acquisition should give them stronger farming potential to reach a critical mass faster, and allow some relevancy lategame.

###### Stats
Evolution Level: 5 -> 4

*Level 4 Stats*
HP: 3604 -> 4064
Atk: 151 -> 185
Defense: 104 -> 136
Sp. Atk: 27 -> 36
Sp. Def: 85 -> 110

###### Whirlpool
Learn level 5 -> 4
Upgrade level 11 -> 10

###### Aqua Tail
Learn level 5 -> 4
Upgrade level 11 -> 10
Cooldown 8s -> 7s

###### Aqua Tail+

title: Patch 1.7.1.2
patchNoteDetails:
Woo! A new year, and a new patch. This is the content patch we've been desperately waiting for - crazy buffs and nerfs, and a chunk of new content. This is a hefty one, so strap in.
 
#### Emblems
Added 52 new emblems (100 to 151)
Added emblem pages to the shop for 5800 coins or 290 gems
###### Black Emblem Bonus
Cooldown reduction: 2/4/8% -> 1/2/4%

###### Flat Attack Emblems

###### Flat Special Attack Emblems
Special Attack Bonus/Loss: 1.5/2.5/3 -> 1.8/2.4/3

###### Critical Hit Emblems
Critical Hit Chance Bonus/Loss: .6/.8/1% -> .3/.5/.6%

Added a "Do not use emblems" option in Pokémon select. (An empty page is no longer required)
 
#### Pokemon

### All Pokemon
###### Unite Buff
OLD: All pokemon got 80% Movement speed, 35% attack speed, 20% CDR, and a 30% HP shield
NEW: All unite moves have their own buffs for 6 seconds, and will be listed on each indivdual pokemon's page when they are confirmed.

### Buzzwole 

Buzzwole was a significant presence on release, then rapidly faded into obscurity. Getting a damage steroid should help get his buggy muscles back on Remoat. What, what do you mean Remoat's gone?

### Mr. Mime

Mike really needed a rework. He was too... weird. They had no real way to buff or nerf Guard Swap to make it more or less viable, it was just too simple and awkward of an ability to have in his kit. This rework completely respecs Mike into a new form. Unfortunately, respeccing costs 2000 Monkey Dollars.

###### Boosted Attack
REWORK: Gains a Boosted Attack at set interval (5s) dealing increased damage and throwing enemies in the air for .5s.

###### Light Screen
NEW: Now briefly stuns on placement 

###### Fake Out
REMOVED: No longer stuns after the shove

###### Barrier
NEW: Now briefly stuns on placement

###### Psychic
REWORK:
Pulses energy dealing damage in an area around themself, any target affected by Guard Swap, and any Barriers or Light Screens. After three pulses, a final pulse will stun those damaged by 1s 
Damage - Final Pulse NEW: 101% SpA + 11 x (Level - 1) + 240
Damage - Pulse NEW: 30.3% SpA x 3 x (Level - 1) + 72
SpDef Shred NEW: 5% each damage tick for 3s

###### Guard Swap -> replaced by Power Swap
Creates a link between the user and the Pokémon this move hits. While the link holds, the user's movement speed increases and its hp is continually restored. The link will continue to hold for as long as the linked Pokémon stay in range or until another target is Power Swapped. If the linked Pokémon is an ally Pokémon, this move increases the movement speed of that Pokémon, increases the amount of damage it deals, and continually restores that Pokémon's HP. If the linked Pokémon is an enemy Pokémon, this move decreases the movement speed of that Pokémon, decreases the amount of damage it deals and deals damage over time to that Pokémon. Damage and healing ticks at a rate of 0.5s

+ Damage: 20% + 2 x (Level - 1) + 50
+ Healing: 20% + 2 x (Level - 1) + 50
+ Damage boost (Ally): 15%
+ Damage debuff (Enemy): 15%
+ Speed buff (Ally and self): 15%
+ Slow (Enemy): 15%

+ Upgrade: Further increases the amount of damage dealt by ally Pokémon and further decreases the amount of damage dealt by opposing Pokémon
+ Damage boost (Ally): 20%
+ Damage debuff (Enemy): 20%

### Tyranitar

A menace in ranked, Ttar was too free a W in non-mirror games. This really doesn't look like it'll be enough to dethrone him, but it's definitely a step in the right direction.

Base: 400 -> 300
###### Tyrannical Rampage
NEW: Attacks during Tyrannical Rampage cannot crit

### Hoopa
###### Basic Attack
Now deals physical damage

### Crustle
###### Stealth Rock
Fixed a bug that caused it to deal higher than expected damage under certain conditions
*NOTE:* This move can no longer crit.

### Lucario
###### Power-up Punch
Fixed a bug that caused the dash to be shorter than intended when fully charged
 
#### HELD ITEMS

I now have 4 in a row on my Bingo sheet. Unfortunately, I think getting five in a row is a little too unlikely given the fifth spot is "removing Lucario from the game".

### Leftovers

Leftovers... is still probably a bad item. But it's a *hell* of a lot more playable now. The extra 120 HP is really respectable, and the out of combat healing triggering at 5s might actually be worthwhile for tanks without a consistent healing option.

Out of combat time: 7s -> 5s
HP OLD: 16 (every 2 level) max 240
HP NEW: 24 (every 2 level) max 360

### Assault Vest

Assault Vest... is also still probably a bad item. But 51 special defense isn't something that should be scoffed at, and the shield on a 5s OoC cooldown... that's really not that bad. It feels like one of those items you only take in draft mode when you know the enemy is leaning too heavily on SpA.

Out of combat time: 7s -> 5s
Sp. Def OLD: 2.8 (every 2 level) max 42
Sp. Def NEW: 3.4 (every 2 level) max 51

### Rocky Helmet

Rocky Helmet... is also still probably a bad item. But 51 defense isn't something that should be scoffed at. It's really not that bad. It feels like one of those items you only take in draft mode when you know the enemy is leaning too heavily on Atk.

Def OLD: 2.8 (every 2 level) max 42
Def NEW: 3.4 (every 2 level) max 51

### Float Stone

This might be the dark horse here. Movement speed is still probably bad, but 5s OoC speed boost is actually not that bad at all. 150 Speed is roughly a 5% speed boost, so this is one of those items that might see more fringe play. You do you, if you like this item, I don't think people should make fun of you for it (as much) anymore.

Out of combat time: 7s -> 5s
Movespeed OLD: 8 (every 2 level) max 120
Movespeed NEW: 10 (every 2 level) max 150

#### Miscellaneous 
New achievement missions have been added. Greedent, Absol, Zeraora achievement difficulty reduced. 
Aeos Energy gained from battle reflects your activity in battle rather than winning or losing.

Spawn Healing reduced - now 15% max hp per half second

title: Patch 1.5.2.7
patchNoteDetails:
Welcome to the June Patch! A few interesting changes here, a few of which are spicy. A lot of lower-tier buffs, and just a sprinkle or higher-tier nerfs. Overall, this is a very nice patch, with a lot of the changes people want. Been a while since we've had one of tho- oh wait, no Lucario nerfs? Welp, grab the pitchforks.
 
#### Pokémon
### Absol
Absol has been completely outshined by other options for many patches now - this well-needed love will allow them to feel less completely worthless in the lategame. Survivability has always been an issue and Lifesteal will give them much higher odds to live after blowing someone up.

###### Stats
Lifesteal (NEW): 5% at 5, 10% at 9

###### Psycho Cut
NOTES: Now procs off Nightslash hits and Unite.

###### Pursuit
Attack Range: Increased by 0.5m

###### Pursuit+

### Crustle
Early game slows on Rock Tomb will allow Crustle to occupy another niche, but they seem to be falling in the Jack of all Trades category. Crustle is never good enough at any role to be played, but better than any other mon at *all* the roles. Unfortunately that likely won't get them picked often competitively.

###### Rock Tomb
Slow (NEW): 40%

###### Rock Tomb+
Slow: 40% -> 60%

###### Stealth Rock
NOTES: Projectile speed increase.

Remember, Zard is not meant to be played as a ranged poker, and TiMi wants you to *know* it. How dare you innovate gameplay.

title: Patch 1.5.1.2
patchNoteDetails:
So, you may notice the patch notes are slightly out of order. This is intentional. The items are listed at the top this time, because their changes are so fundamentally impactful to the meta, that the rest of the patch notes are virtually worthless without their understanding.

Now that I've sufficiently hyped it up, enjoy the first actually meaningful balance patch we've had in... hm. Forever?

Also of note, XP seems to have been changed. We will investigate and update you on the developments in the Mathcord.
 
#### Held Items

The two best Held Items in the game just took an axeing. Buddy Barrier shields are no longer OP, and Score Shields are no longer impenetrable. The meta is changing, everyone! Usher in the Era of Diverse Items!

###### Buddy Barrier
*Passive HP:* 200/400/600 -> 150/300/450
*Shield:* 20%/30%/40% MaxHP -> 15%/20%/25% Max HP
NOTES: Multiple Buddy Barrier shields no longer stack on top of one another.

###### Score Shield
Internal Cooldown: 6s -> 20s
NOTES: The shield is now consumed *before* any other shields.
NOTES: Shield amount now increases with level? *(EDIT)* This was mentioned in translation from the Japan Twitter Account. We're not exactly sure what this means yet. Stay tuned.
*(EDIT)* Shield values were datamined and are as follows:
*Shield:*
Levels 1-9: 5% Max HP + 15 × (Level - 1) + 15
Levels 10-19: 7.5% Max HP + 22 × (Level - 1) + 22
Levels 20-30: 10% Max HP + 30 × (Level - 1) + 30
 
#### Pokémon

### Greedent
Fast squirrel go less fast.

###### Covet
NOTES: CC is no longer just flat-out removed, it's just converted to a slow instead.
NOTES: Attack scaling altered. Now uses Atk instead of SpA scaling.
_Damage:_
Ratio: 75% SpA -> 55% Atk
Per Level: 5 -> 4
Base: 250 -> 180

Per Level: 6 -> 5
Base: 280 -> 200

### Blissey
Imagine the countless possibilities of egg-based puns I could have used in this section. Just imagine how awful they could have been. Let your imagination run wild. You can be the author of your own pun groans.

###### Soft Boiled and Egg Bomb
NOTES: Soft Boiled has a cooldown of 12s while Egg Bomb is not learned. Egg Bomb has a cooldown of 9s while Soft Boiled is not learned. When both are learned, they each share an 8s cooldown.
*(NEW)* Both moves now grant two egg charges each upon learning them. These moves now share a charge reserve.

###### Soft Boiled+
NOTES: Now unlocks at level 10 (prev 12). 
NOTES: No longer has additional cooldown reduction.
NOTES: Grants +1 egg charges upon learning.

###### Safeguard+
NOTES: Now unlocks at level 10 (prev 12).

###### Egg Bomb+

This poor excuse of a monolith has been a terror in the Unite scene for a bit, and really needed a bit of a kneecapping. This change definitely is going to hurt Dragon Pulse, but whether it's actually enough to stop this glorified stone is something we'll have to wait to see.
Laz note: Dragon pulse probably deserved the nerf but we didn't deserve having to calculate dragon pulse again, these numbers will be out later.

title: Patch 1.4.1.7
patchNoteDetails:
PREFACE: There's a storage bug some people are encountering. To fix it, [check out this tweet from Peek!](https://twitter.com/peekmp/status/1503356704557191172) It has the details on how to fix the bug :)

Well, looks like the long awaited balance patch is here! We've got some really unique changes this time. As always, a mixed bag. Some really interesting nerfs and buffs in this patch, it'll be amusing to see how it matures.

### Blastoise
Rapid Spin is spinnier. Water Spout is spoutier.
###### Water Spout:
Laz Note: The official patch notes lists this as a Rapid Spin change, we think it's better described as a Water Spout change.
NOTES: Water Spout's duration during rapid spin has been increased to last the entirety of Rapid Spin.
Max hits during Rapid Spin: 8 -> 17

### Mr. Mime
Mr. Mime has been in a pretty solid spot for a while and damage reduction on Confusion *shouldn't* do too much. Buffing Psychic and Guard Swap might make Mime's secondary sets more appealing as well.
###### Guard Swap:
NOTES: Now lasts as long as your target is in range, breaks after target moves too far away or the move is used again on another target.
NOTES: Damage and healing triggers every half second instead of every second.
NOTES: There was no formula change here, just the tick rates :)
###### Confusion:
NOTES: This is a damage reduction, overall.
_Damage:_
Ratio: 170.4% -> 175%
Scale: 20 -> 16
Base: 460 -> 400
_Damage: (Wall Hit)_
Ratio: 204.48% -> 200%
Scale: 23 -> 16
Base: 552 -> 450
###### Confusion+:
NOTES: Damage Reduced.
_Damage:_
Ratio: 213% -> 191%
Scale: 23 -> 16
Base: 510 -> 450
_Damage: (Wall Hit)_
Ratio: 266.6% -> 219%
Scale: 27 -> 21
Base: 612 -> 500
###### Psychic:
NOTES: Mime can now attack during the cast.

### Cramorant
The Dive change is power neutral for Dive in scenarios where you're using Dive for damage, and neutral-to-positive in circumstances where you're running away. It's strictly negative in circumstances where you're trying to use it for burst damage. It's also only on the next auto after the dive, so you'll need to position yourself differently. Overall this is a nerf to Chad Cramorant, and a buff to Coward Cramorant.
###### Dive: 
NOTES: Smoother animation
_Damage:_
Ratio: 78% -> 45%
Scale: 14 -> 9
Base: 320 -> 180
NEW: Adds bonus damage to the first target hit by your boosted attack after dive
_Damage Increase:_
Ratio: 38%
Scale: 8
Base: 145
###### Dive+:
_Damage:_
Ratio: 86% -> 42%
Scale: 16 -> 16
Base: 360 -> 185
###### Air Slash:

### Alolan Ninetales
Little quality of life changes, nothing major. Aurora Veil got a cute little buff that makes it less clunky to use. Ninetales should be graceful and always flowing - having a small dash instead of stopping abruptly to Veil is a pretty cute way to get that going. Buff to strength *and* aesthetics, nice!
###### Boosted Attack:
NOTES: Smoother, faster animation
Laz note: There's either a temporary attack speed buff given while you have a boosted auto active or Ninetales' base attack speed was increased - it's likely the former. Either way this leads to an increase in the amount of attacks you get within Aurora Veil.
###### Aurora Veil:
NOTES: Now is also a dash and causes boosted attacks to heal while in Veil.

title: Patch 1.4.1.2
patchNoteDetails:
Do you know how long it takes us to find all these changes? We started around 10pm last night, and are publishing around 3am. Five hours of a team of people grinding away to get you the most detailed numbers we can.

Thanks to the Mathcord Contributors and Icarus Twine for helping with today's notes. Couldn't have done it this quickly without you lot.

### Dragonite
Dragonite was the best objective-securing mon in the game. This hasn't changed. Their Hyper Beam got a numerical nerf, but the %MaxHP damage is still the same as before. Their Unite doubled in cooldown though, so it'll make them slightly less toxic at rushing backdoor fifties.

###### Stats
Attack Range: 6m -> 5.5m
###### Hyper Beam:
_Stage 0:_
Ratio: 220% -> 187%
Per Level: 9 -> 8
Base: 410 -> 350
_Stage 1:_
Ratio: 264% -> 224.4%
Per Level: 11 -> 10
Base: 492 -> 420
_Stage 2:_
Ratio: 308% -> 261.8%
Per Level: 13 -> 11
Base: 574 -> 490
_Stage 3:_
Ratio: 352% -> 299.2%
Per Level: 15 -> 13
Base: 656 -> 560
###### ExtremeSpeed
Range: 5.5m -> 6m
Cooldown: 9s -> 7s
###### Unite
Energy Cost: 30000 -> 60000

### Tsareena
Tsareena needed a kick in the knees. One of the strongest mons in higher end competitive, Tsareena just did way too much damage for free. This nerf to Triple Axel and Grassy Glide will hopefully put them more reasonably in line with the rest of the all-rounders. Maybe some people will even look at the buffed Stomp now too!
###### Stats
Attack Speed: Increased basic attack speed.
###### Triple Axel
NOTE: The bonus attack speed from the Triple Axel buff has been reduced.
Ratio: 71.3 -> 61.74%
Per Level: 4 -> 3
Base: 96 -> 78

Per Level: 7 -> 5
Base: 153 -> 125

Ratio: 82% -> 71%
Per Level: 5 -> 4
Base: 110 -> 90
_Queenly Majesty Damage:_
Ratio: 131.2% -> 113.6%
Per Level: 7 -> 6
Base: 176 -> 144
###### Stomp
_Heal:_
Ratio: 37.5% -> 60%
Per Level: 2 -> 4
Base: 75 -> 60
###### Grassy Glide
Cooldown: 6.5s -> 8s
NOTES: Slow removed

### Talonflame
Fly is now a much less powerful escape tool, and about the same strength engage tool. It does less damage, but has a slightly lower cooldown if you're brawling with it. Aerial Ace healing now provides Talon a much more interesting build path as well. It'll be interesting to see if it changes anything.
###### Fly
Ratio: 255% ->  221%
Per Level: 24 -> 21
Base: 1050 -> 910
Cooldown: 9s -> 12s
NOTES: (NEW) If you hit a least one enemy Pokémon, the cooldown is reduced by 40%
###### Aerial Ace
_Heal:_ (NEW)
Ratio: 50%
Per Level: 0
Base: 45

### Lucario
MORE NERFS MORE NERFS MORE NERFS

Per Level: 10 -> 8

Base: 56 -> 42
###### Close Combat

_Heal:_ (NEW)
Ratio: 15%
Per Level: 0
Base: 40
###### Power-Up-Punch
BUGFIX: When spamming attack after using PuP, sometimes the damage from PuP was negated. This has been fixed.

### Charizard
Okay, let's give a little preface. Charizard is *slightly* slower when using a basic attack. It wasn't much, but it was enough to make chasing a little irritating. That slight slow has been all but removed. That's what the "slow" is under Basic Attack. It's a self-slow, and the lower number is good. All of these changes are buffs to Zard. Paired with the new moving fire moves, Charizard is much slipperier now.
###### Basic Attack
Slow: 15% -> 5%
###### Flamethrower
NOTES: (NEW) Can now be cast while moving
###### Fire Blast
NOTES: (NEW) Can now be cast while moving
###### Unite
Range: 5000 -> 6000

### Decidueye
Decidueye just got a major change here, making them a much scarier long-distance sniper. No longer are they a one-shot-chump, they now get three the attempts to snipe you, and from an even *longer* distance.
###### Stats
Lifesteal: 0/0/0/0/5/5/5/5/10/10/15/15/20/20/20 -> 0/0/0/0/5/5/10/10/15/15/20/20/25/25/25
###### Spirit Shackle

###### Razor Leaf

Base: 38 -> 46
###### Leaf Storm
NOTES: Hindrance buff

### Machamp
Machamp's a little tankier, a bit more consistent, a bit more damaging, and bit more ranged. Jack of all trades buff, this is. The Submission change in particular is of note, such that Machamp is no longer worthless if the opponent pops a Full Heal.
###### Stats:
Lifesteal: 0/0/0/0/5/5/5/5/10/10/10/10/15/15/15 -> 0/0/0/0/5/5/10/10/15/15/15/15/20/20/20
###### Karate Chop

Per Level: 15 -> 17
Base: 270 -> 320
###### Dynamic Punch
Range: 4000 -> 5000
###### Submission
NOTES: Damage and self-buff effects are now activated even if the opposing mon isn't CCed 

### Gengar
Gengar needed something to pull them out of the gutter - shuffling around moves and nerfing Hex likely wouldn't do much - but the Dream Eater change is interesting enough that it could spawn a whole new style of play. Assassins love resets.
###### General
NOTES: Gengar's learn order has been swapped. Moves learned at 5 are now learned at 7, and vice versa. The same is true with the upgraded moves.
###### Hex

Per Level: 5 -> 3
Base: 220 -> 150

NOTES: (NEW) KOing a opposing Pokemon resets Cooldown

### Garchomp
Garchomp has sat at the bottom of the barrel for a while. They've had difficulty staying in fights and surviving them. Allowing Dig to offer a bit more dive potential might help in the long run. Oh also, all abilities slow now, so that's pretty nice.
###### Dig

###### Dragon Rush+

###### Dragon Claw
NOTES: (NEW) Slows

### Gardevoir
Stealth buffs! Or bugs. Probably the latter. But Garde mains rejoice anyways.
###### Future Sight
NOTES: (NEW) Cooldown reduction now applied to base Future Sight as well (75% reduction when landing a Future Sight).

### Zeraora
BUGFIXES:
###### Volt Absorb
Fixed an issue where after Zeraora was knocked out and respawned, Volt Absorb’s effect was still active.
###### Wild Charge
Fixed an issue where the increase in the number of Spark attacks was canceled by Full Heal.

### Battle items
Attack Speed is an impossible stat to properly quantify. We're still digging into these numbers, they're currently understood by nobody. Something screwy is going on with Attack Speed, and we just haven't cracked it yet. Once we do, we'll be glad to explain everything.
###### XAttack

### Venusaur:
Verdant Anger took a pretty interesting... change. It's not really fair to call this a buff or nerf, it's just... different. It depends a lot on how this ult will be used, but it'll miss middle targets a bit less often now. Petal Dance also received a hit - Venusaur's copy of "Gas Gas Gas" unfortunately got lost in the move, so whe'll have to live with a mixtape of lofi hiphop gifted to him by a well-meaning aunt instead.

title: Patch 1.3.1.2
patchNoteDetails:
Some people didn't like when I added flavorful paragraphs to the notes midway through. I still have this opening paragraph, though! Take that! Anyways, this is one of the larger patches that has come out in a while, and a ton of changes are happening. Many people have been saying these changes are overdue, and many of these changes seem to bolster the weaker mons and somewhat hurt the stronger ones. Perhaps this isn't enough - but at the very least, it's definitely a step in the right direction. Fingers crossed that Lucario isn't pick/ban in every esports match anymore.

title: Patch 1.1.1.6
patchNoteDetails:
So, before we get into this, I want to discuss Damage Formulas for a bit. Nearly all abilities scale off of three things - what we call the RSB. The Ratio is the amount the ability scales off your Attack stat (or your SpA stat if you're a Special mon). The Slider is the amount of damage you deal extra every level. The Base is a flat, unchanging amount of damage that the move will always deal.

The basic damage formula is **R\*A + S\*L + B**. It should be very easy to figure out how much damage a move will do at a certain level, with a certain attack stat, before defenses. Remember that everything rounds down!

With that out of the way, here are the 1.1.1.6 Patch Notes.

***

    Changes are being made to some Pokémon stats and moves to adjust the balance of Unite Battles.
